game_name: Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego?

danger_signals.visual_form: Brief animation of V.I.L.E. henchman lurking on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Player has correctly tracked criminal to current location; danger is imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Animations of knife being thrown or gun being fired
danger_signals.meaning: Thief is actively defending themselves; player is at immediate risk of attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Witness dialogue instructing player to 'be careful'
danger_signals.meaning: Criminal is present and hostile; normal investigation yields safety warnings instead of clues

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen clock display advancing toward deadline
danger_signals.meaning: Failure to apprehend criminal within time window results in escape

safety_signals.visual_form: Witness provides clues about thief identity or next destination
safety_signals.meaning: Player has traveled to a location where the suspect has been spotted; investigation is productive

safety_signals.visual_form: Interpol database display confirms arrest warrant for single suspect
safety_signals.meaning: Player has gathered sufficient evidence; legal authority to apprehend confirmed

safety_signals.visual_form: Police chase animation and apprehension sequence
safety_signals.meaning: Case solved; player advances in rank and difficulty
Safe to continue investigation; criminal has not visited this location

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player's home base; Interpol database terminals at each city location

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Henchman sprite animation represents generic V.I.L.E. operatives
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Animations of criminal activity (knife throwing, gunfire) at thief locations; criminal presence confirmed by witness sightings

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Carmen Sandiego name/identity serves as ultimate target
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Carmen is final boss; encounters with her operatives precede final confrontation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final case in game progression; apprehending Carmen grants Hall of Fame status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alert notification describing stolen artifact and initial location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must immediately begin investigation and travel sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: V.I.L.E. henchman lurking animation appears on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Witness provides useful clues; player can narrow suspect profile

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No animation; witness has nothing to report
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must backtrack and select different travel destination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interpol database confirms single warrant match
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains authorization to apprehend; incorrect entry eliminates all suspects and requires restart

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Knife/gunfire animations; witness warnings instead of clues
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must select correct investigation site with valid warrant to trigger apprehension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: On-screen clock reaches deadline; thief escapes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Case fails; player must restart with new suspect

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clock advances to late hour; automatic sleep sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player loses several in-game hours; investigation resumes next morning

environmental_storytelling.example: 30 countries represented by prominent cities; player must use geographic knowledge to predict criminal's escape route based on real-world geography
Each city location contains multiple witnesses with different information; correct locations yield identity/destination clues; wrong locations yield no information
Each case begins with alert of famous artwork theft from specific location; players must understand what makes locations geographically significant to predict criminal movement

environmental_storytelling.technique: Interpol database as faction authority marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Presence of Interpol terminals in every city reinforces ACME Detective Agency's international reach and legitimacy as law enforcement

environmental_storytelling.technique: Criminal animation as danger presence indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: V.I.L.E. henchman lurking animation serves as environmental confirmation that player has correctly deduced criminal location through geographic reasoning
Geography questions at each promotion act as both copy-protection and narrative reinforcement that player's knowledge directly enables career advancement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Lurks on screen; animation confirms thief proximity
Moves between countries; witnesses report sightings; leaves no trace at unvisited locations
Throws knives and fires guns; witnesses warn player to be careful; remains hidden in one of three investigation sites
Operates as ultimate target across all cases; apprehension grants Hall of Fame achievement
Reports nothing suspicious; refuses to provide clues
Provides clues about criminal identity or next destination
Cannot issue warrant; must continue investigation at other locations
Can confront thief but apprehension fails; case lost

notes: This is an educational geography game where visual manifestations are sparse and minimal. The core communication system relies on text-based witness dialogue and animated confirmations rather than environmental storytelling. The Master System version introduces action-based sprite movement and attack animations, creating more visceral danger signals through player damage and time loss from evasion. V.I.L.E. is depicted as a distributed criminal network rather than a unified faction with consistent visual identity—their presence is communicated through the absence of witnesses and through brief combat animations. The game's political situation is simplified: ACME (law enforcement) vs. V.I.L.E. (criminals), with no faction ambiguity or moral complexity. World-state is primarily communicated through clock mechanics and database UI rather than environmental change.
